briefdescription.para: The default version of XML used: 1.0.
Special constant required for SAX2 handlers.
Thread-local setting to enable validation.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 0.

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to disable warnings.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 1.

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to ignore some whitespace.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 1.

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to store line numbers.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 0, but is always enabled after setting parser options.

xrefdescription.para: Shouldn't be needed when using parser options.

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to enable loading of external DTDs.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 0.

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to enable pedantic warnings.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 0.

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to enable entity substitution.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 0.

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to disable indenting when formatting output.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 1.

xrefdescription.para: Use the xmlsave.h API with option XML_SAVE_NO_INDENT.

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to change the indent string.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to two spaces.

xrefdescription.para: Use the xmlsave.h API and xmlSaveSetIndentString().

briefdescription.para: Thread-local setting to disable empty tags when serializing.

detaileddescription.para: Defaults to 0.

xrefdescription.para: Use the xmlsave.h API with option XML_SAVE_NO_EMPTY.

briefdescription.para: not well-formed
not namespace-well-formed
DTD validation failed.
catastrophic failure like OOM or I/O error
Status after parsing a document.
unknown
main document
external DTD
external general entity
external parameter entity
XIncluded document.
XIncluded text.
Resource type for resource loaders.
The input buffer won't be changed during parsing.
The input buffer is zero-terminated.

detaileddescription.para: (Note that the zero byte shouldn't be included in buffer size.)

briefdescription.para: Uncompress gzipped file input.
Allow network access.

detaileddescription.para: Unused internally.

briefdescription.para: Allow system catalog to resolve URIs.
Flags for parser input.
Enable "recovery" mode which allows non-wellformed documents.

detaileddescription.para: How this mode behaves exactly is unspecified and may change without further notice. Use of this feature is DISCOURAGED.
Not supported by the push parser.

briefdescription.para: Despite the confusing name, this option enables substitution of entities.

detaileddescription.para: The resulting tree won't contain any entity reference nodes.

This option also enables loading of external entities (both general and parameter entities) which is dangerous. If you process untrusted data, it's recommended to set the XML_PARSE_NO_XXE option to disable loading of external entities.

briefdescription.para: Enables loading of an external DTD and the loading and substitution of external parameter entities.

detaileddescription.para: Has no effect if XML_PARSE_NO_XXE is set.

briefdescription.para: Adds default attributes from the DTD to the result document.

detaileddescription.para: Implies XML_PARSE_DTDLOAD, but loading of external content can be disabled with XML_PARSE_NO_XXE.

briefdescription.para: This option enables DTD validation which requires to load external DTDs and external entities (both general and parameter entities) unless XML_PARSE_NO_XXE was set.

detaileddescription.para: DTD validation is vulnerable to algorithmic complexity attacks and should never be enabled with untrusted input.

briefdescription.para: Disable error and warning reports to the error handlers.

detaileddescription.para: Errors are still accessible with xmlCtxtGetLastError().

briefdescription.para: Disable warning reports.
Enable some pedantic warnings.
Remove some whitespace from the result document.

detaileddescription.para: Where to remove whitespace depends on DTD element declarations or a broken heuristic with unfixable bugs. Use of this option is DISCOURAGED.
Not supported by the push parser.

briefdescription.para: Always invoke the deprecated SAX1 startElement and endElement handlers.

xrefdescription.para: This option will be removed in a future version.

briefdescription.para: Enable XInclude processing.

detaileddescription.para: This option only affects the xmlTextReader and XInclude interfaces.

briefdescription.para: Disable network access with the built-in HTTP or FTP clients.

detaileddescription.para: After the last built-in network client was removed in 2.15, this option has no effect expect for being passed on to custom resource loaders.

briefdescription.para: Create a document without interned strings, making all strings separate memory allocations.
Remove redundant namespace declarations from the result document.
Output normal text nodes instead of CDATA nodes.
Don't generate XInclude start/end nodes when expanding inclusions.

detaileddescription.para: This option only affects the xmlTextReader and XInclude interfaces.

briefdescription.para: Store small strings directly in the node struct to save memory.
Use old Name productions from before XML 1.0 Fifth Edition.

xrefdescription.para: This option will be removed in a future version.

briefdescription.para: Don't fix up XInclude xml:base URIs.

detaileddescription.para: This option only affects the xmlTextReader and XInclude interfaces.

briefdescription.para: Relax some internal limits.

detaileddescription.para: Maximum size of text nodes, tags, comments, processing instructions, CDATA sections, entity values
normal: 10M huge: 1B
Maximum size of names, system literals, pubid literals
normal: 50K huge: 10M
Maximum nesting depth of elements
normal: 256 huge: 2048
Maximum nesting depth of entities
normal: 20 huge: 40

briefdescription.para: Enable an unspecified legacy mode for SAX parsers.

xrefdescription.para: This option will be removed in a future version.

briefdescription.para: Ignore the encoding in the XML declaration.

detaileddescription.para: This option is mostly unneeded these days. The only effect is to enforce UTF-8 decoding of ASCII-like data.

briefdescription.para: Enable reporting of line numbers larger than 65535.
Disables loading of external DTDs or entities.
Enable input decompression.

detaileddescription.para: Setting this option is discouraged to avoid zip bombs.

briefdescription.para: Disables the global system XML catalog.
Enable XML catalog processing instructions.
Force the parser to ignore IDs.
This is the set of XML parser options that can be passed to xmlReadDoc, xmlCtxtSetOptions and other functions.
Multithreading support.
Serialization support.
Push parser.
XML Reader.
Streaming patterns.
XML Writer.
Legacy SAX1 API.
DTD validation.
HTML parser.
Legacy symbols.
Canonical XML.
XML Catalogs.
XPath.
XPointer.
XInclude.
iconv
Built-in ISO-8859-X.
Regular expressions.
XML Schemas.
Schematron.
Loadable modules.
Debugging API.
GZIP compression.
ICU.
RELAXNG, since 2.14.
Used to examine the existence of features that can be enabled or disabled at compile-time.

detaileddescription.para: They used to be called XML_FEATURE_xxx but this clashed with Expat

memberdef.definition: typedef xmlParserErrors(* xmlResourceLoader) (void *ctxt, const char *url, const char *publicId, xmlResourceType type, xmlParserInputFlags flags, xmlParserInput **out)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctxt, const char *url, const char *publicId, xmlResourceType type, xmlParserInputFlags flags, xmlParserInput **out)
memberdef.name: xmlResourceLoader

briefdescription.para: Callback for custom resource loaders.

detaileddescription.para: flags can contain XML_INPUT_UNZIP and XML_INPUT_NETWORK.
The URL is resolved using XML catalogs before being passed to the callback.
On success, out should be set to a new parser input object and XML_ERR_OK should be returned.

parameterdescription.para: URL or system ID to load

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code.

memberdef.definition: typedef xmlParserInput *(* resolveEntitySAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.name: resolveEntitySAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback to resolve external entities.

detaileddescription.para: This is only used to load DTDs. The preferred way to install custom resolvers is xmlCtxtSetResourceLoader.

parameterdescription.para: The public identifier of the entity
The system identifier of the entity (URL)

simplesect.para: the xmlParserInput if inlined or NULL for DOM behaviour.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* internalSubsetSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.name: internalSubsetSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for the internal subset.

parameterdescription.para: the system identifier (e.g. filename or URL)

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* externalSubsetSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.name: externalSubsetSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for the external subset.

parameterdescription.para: the system identifier (e.g. filename or URL)

briefdescription.para: SAX callback to look up a general entity by name.

simplesect.para: the xmlEntity if found.

briefdescription.para: SAX callback to look up a parameter entity by name.

simplesect.para: the xmlEntity if found.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* entityDeclSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, int type, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId, xmlChar *content)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, int type, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId, xmlChar *content)
memberdef.name: entityDeclSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for entity declarations.

parameterdescription.para: The public ID of the entity
The system ID of the entity

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* notationDeclSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.name: notationDeclSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for notation declarations.

parameterdescription.para: The public ID of the notation
The system ID of the notation

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* attributeDeclSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *elem, const xmlChar *fullname, int type, int def, const xmlChar *defaultValue, xmlEnumeration *tree)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctx, const xmlChar *elem, const xmlChar *fullname, int type, int def, const xmlChar *defaultValue, xmlEnumeration *tree)
memberdef.name: attributeDeclSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for attribute declarations.

parameterdescription.para: the tree of enumerated value set

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* elementDeclSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, int type, xmlElementContent *content)
memberdef.name: elementDeclSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for element declarations.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* unparsedEntityDeclSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId, const xmlChar *notationName)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId, const xmlChar *notationName)
memberdef.name: unparsedEntityDeclSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for unparsed entity declarations.

parameterdescription.para: The public ID of the entity
The system ID of the entity

briefdescription.para: This callback receives the "document locator" at startup, which is always the global xmlDefaultSAXLocator.

detaileddescription.para: Everything is available on the context, so this is useless in our case.

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for start of document.
SAX callback for end of document.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* startElementSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar **atts)
memberdef.name: startElementSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for start tags.

parameterdescription.para: The element name, including namespace prefix
An array of name/value attributes pairs, NULL terminated

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for end tags.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* attributeSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *value)
memberdef.name: attributeSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: Callback for attributes.

parameterdescription.para: The attribute name, including namespace prefix

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for entity references.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* charactersSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *ch, int len)
memberdef.name: charactersSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for character data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* ignorableWhitespaceSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *ch, int len)
memberdef.name: ignorableWhitespaceSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for "ignorable" whitespace.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* processingInstructionSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *target, const xmlChar *data)
memberdef.name: processingInstructionSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for processing instructions.
SAX callback for comments.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* cdataBlockSAXFunc) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *value, int len)
memberdef.name: cdataBlockSAXFunc

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for CDATA sections.
SAX callback for warning messages.

parameterdescription.para: extra parameters for the message display

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for error messages.

parameterdescription.para: extra parameters for the message display

briefdescription.para: SAX callback for fatal error messages.

parameterdescription.para: extra parameters for the message display

briefdescription.para: SAX callback to get standalone status.

simplesect.para: 1 if true

briefdescription.para: SAX callback to get internal subset status.

simplesect.para: 1 if true

briefdescription.para: SAX callback to get external subset status.

simplesect.para: 1 if true

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* startElementNsSAX2Func) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *localname, const xmlChar *prefix, const xmlChar *URI, int nb_namespaces, const xmlChar **namespaces, int nb_attributes, int nb_defaulted, const xmlChar **attributes)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctx, const xmlChar *localname, const xmlChar *prefix, const xmlChar *URI, int nb_namespaces, const xmlChar **namespaces, int nb_attributes, int nb_defaulted, const xmlChar **attributes)
memberdef.name: startElementNsSAX2Func

briefdescription.para: SAX2 callback for start tags.

detaileddescription.para: It provides the namespace information for the element, as well as the new namespace declarations on the element.

parameterdescription.para: the local name of the element
the element namespace prefix if available
the element namespace name if available
number of namespace definitions on that node
pointer to the array of prefix/URI pairs namespace definitions
the number of attributes on that node
the number of defaulted attributes. The defaulted ones are at the end of the array
pointer to the array of (localname/prefix/URI/value/end) attribute values.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* endElementNsSAX2Func) (void *ctx, const xmlChar *localname, const xmlChar *prefix, const xmlChar *URI)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *ctx, const xmlChar *localname, const xmlChar *prefix, const xmlChar *URI)
memberdef.name: endElementNsSAX2Func

briefdescription.para: SAX2 callback for end tags.

detaileddescription.para: It provides the namespace information for the element.

parameterdescription.para: the local name of the element
the element namespace prefix if available
the element namespace name if available

briefdescription.para: SAX handler, version 1.

memberdef.definition: typedef xmlParserInput *(* xmlExternalEntityLoader) (const char *URL, const char *publicId, xmlParserCtxt *context)
memberdef.name: xmlExternalEntityLoader

briefdescription.para: Callback for external entity loader.

detaileddescription.para: The URL is not resolved using XML catalogs before being passed to the callback.

parameterdescription.para: The URL or system ID of the resource requested
The public ID of the resource requested (optional)

simplesect.para: the entity input parser or NULL on error.

briefdescription.para: Constant string describing the version of the library used at run-time.
Initialization function for the XML parser.

detaileddescription.para: For older versions, it's recommended to call this function once from the main thread before using the library in multithreaded programs.
Since 2.14.0, there's no distinction between threads. It should be unnecessary to call this function.

briefdescription.para: Free global memory allocations.

detaileddescription.para: This function is named somewhat misleadingly. It does not clean up parser state but frees global memory allocated by various components of the library.

Since 2.9.11, cleanup is performed automatically on most platforms and there's no need at all for manual cleanup. This includes all compilers and platforms that support GCC-style destructor attributes as well as Windows DLLs.
This function should only be used to avoid false positives from memory leak checkers if automatic cleanup isn't possible, for example with static builds on MSVC.
WARNING: xmlCleanupParser is not thread-safe. If this function is called and any threads that could make calls into libxml2 are still running, memory corruption is likely to occur.

No library calls must be made (from any thread) after calling this function. In general, this function should only be called right before the whole process exits. Calling this function too early will lead to memory corruption.

xrefdescription.para: This function is a no-op. Call xmlCleanupParser to free global state but see the warnings there. xmlCleanupParser should be only called once at program exit. In most cases, you don't have call cleanup functions at all.
This function was internal and is deprecated.

parameterdescription.para: an indicative size for the lookahead

simplesect.para: -1 as this is an error to use it.

briefdescription.para: This function increase the input for the parser.

detaileddescription.para: It tries to preserve pointers to the input buffer, and keep already read data

parameterdescription.para: an indicative size for the lookahead

simplesect.para: the amount of char read, or -1 in case of error, 0 indicate the end of this entity

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree if the file was wellformed, NULL otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory block and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: an pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Set and return the previous value for default entity support.

xrefdescription.para: Use the modern options API with XML_PARSE_NOENT.

simplesect.para: the last value for 0 for no substitution, 1 for substitution.

briefdescription.para: Set and return the previous value for default blanks text nodes support.

xrefdescription.para: Use the modern options API with XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS.

simplesect.para: the last value for 0 for no substitution, 1 for substitution.

briefdescription.para: Blocks further parser processing.
Set and return the previous value for enabling pedantic warnings.

xrefdescription.para: Use the modern options API with XML_PARSE_PEDANTIC.

simplesect.para: the last value for 0 for no substitution, 1 for substitution.

briefdescription.para: Has no effect.
Set per-thread default value.

simplesect.para: the old value

briefdescription.para: Set per-thread default value.

xrefdescription.para: Whitespace is kept by default. Use xmlParserOption XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS to remove whitespace.

simplesect.para: the old value

briefdescription.para: Set per-thread default value.

simplesect.para: the old value

briefdescription.para: Set per-thread default value.
Set per-thread default value.

simplesect.para: the old value

briefdescription.para: Set per-thread default value.

simplesect.para: the old value

briefdescription.para: Set per-thread default value.

simplesect.para: the old value

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: In the case the document is not Well Formed, a attempt to build a tree is tried anyway
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT | XML_PARSE_RECOVER.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL in case of failure

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory block and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: In the case the document is not Well Formed, an attempt to build a tree is tried anyway
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT | XML_PARSE_RECOVER.

parameterdescription.para: an pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time. In the case the document is not Well Formed, it attempts to build a tree anyway
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT | XML_PARSE_RECOVER.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL in case of failure

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document and invoke the SAX handlers.

detaileddescription.para: This is useful if you're only interested in custom SAX callbacks. If you want a document tree, use xmlCtxtParseDocument.

simplesect.para: 0, -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Parse a general parsed entity An external general parsed entity is well-formed if it matches the production labeled extParsedEnt.

simplesect.para: 0, -1 in case of error. the parser context is augmented as a result of the parsing.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and call the given SAX handler routines.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: The user data returned on SAX callbacks

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or a error number otherwise

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory buffer and call the given SAX handler routines.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: The user data returned on SAX callbacks
the length of the XML document in bytes

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or a error number otherwise

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar
work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read no Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSAXHandler *sax, const char *buffer, int size, int recovery)
memberdef.name: xmlSAXParseMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory block and use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback.

detaileddescription.para: If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: an pointer to a char array
work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read not Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSAXHandler *sax, const char *buffer, int size, int recovery, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlSAXParseMemoryWithData

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory block and use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback.

detaileddescription.para: If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.
User data (void *) is stored within the parser context in the context's _private member, so it is available nearly everywhere in libxml

parameterdescription.para: an pointer to a char array
work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read no Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time. It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read no Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: Automatic support for ZLIB/Compress compressed document is provided by default if found at compile-time. It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.
User data (void *) is stored within the parser context in the context's _private member, so it is available nearly everywhere in libxml

parameterdescription.para: work in recovery mode, i.e. tries to read no Well Formed documents

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML external entity out of context and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: It use the given SAX function block to handle the parsing callback. If sax is NULL, fallback to the default DOM tree building routines.

Deprecated
Don't use. [78] extParsedEnt ::= TextDecl? content This correspond to a "Well Balanced" chunk
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML external entity out of context and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: [78] extParsedEnt ::= TextDecl? content This correspond to a "Well Balanced" chunk
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse a DTD.

detaileddescription.para: Option XML_PARSE_DTDLOAD should be enabled in the parser context to make external entities work.

simplesect.para: the resulting xmlDtd or NULL in case of error. input will be freed by the function in any case.

briefdescription.para: Validate a document.

detaileddescription.para: Like xmlValidateDocument but uses the parser context's error handler.
Option XML_PARSE_DTDLOAD should be enabled in the parser context to make external entities work.

simplesect.para: 1 if valid or 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Validate a document against a DTD.

detaileddescription.para: Like xmlValidateDtd but uses the parser context's error handler.

simplesect.para: 1 if valid or 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Load and parse an external subset.

simplesect.para: the resulting xmlDtd or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Load and parse an external subset.

simplesect.para: the resulting xmlDtd or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Load and parse a DTD.

parameterdescription.para: the SAX handler block or NULL

simplesect.para: the resulting xmlDtd or NULL in case of error. input will be freed by the function in any case.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, int depth, const xmlChar *string, xmlNode **lst)
memberdef.name: xmlParseBalancedChunkMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse a well-balanced chunk of an XML document called by the parser The allowed sequence for the Well Balanced Chunk is the one defined by the content production in the XML grammar:

detaileddescription.para: [43] content ::= (element | CharData | Reference | CDSect | PI | Comment)* This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: the document the chunk pertains to (must not be NULL)
The user data returned on SAX callbacks (possibly NULL)
the input string in UTF8 or ISO-Latin (zero terminated)
the return value for the set of parsed nodes

simplesect.para: 0 if the chunk is well balanced, -1 in case of args problem and the parser error code otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlNode *node, const char *data, int datalen, int options, xmlNode **lst)
memberdef.name: xmlParseInNodeContext

briefdescription.para: Parse a well-balanced chunk of an XML document within the context (DTD, namespaces, etc ...) of the given node.

detaileddescription.para: The allowed sequence for the data is a Well Balanced Chunk defined by the content production in the XML grammar: [43] content ::= (element | CharData | Reference | CDSect | PI | Comment)* This function assumes the encoding of node's document which is typically not what you want. A better alternative is xmlCtxtParseContent.

parameterdescription.para: the input string length in bytes
the return value for the set of parsed nodes

simplesect.para: XML_ERR_OK if the chunk is well balanced, and the parser error code otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, int depth, const xmlChar *string, xmlNode **lst, int recover)
memberdef.name: xmlParseBalancedChunkMemoryRecover

briefdescription.para: Parse a well-balanced chunk of an XML document.

detaileddescription.para: The allowed sequence for the Well Balanced Chunk is the one defined by the content production in the XML grammar: [43] content ::= (element | CharData | Reference | CDSect | PI | Comment)* In case recover is set to 1, the nodelist will not be empty even if the parsed chunk is not well balanced, assuming the parsing succeeded to some extent.
This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: the document the chunk pertains to (must not be NULL)
The user data returned on SAX callbacks (possibly NULL)
the input string in UTF8 or ISO-Latin (zero terminated)
the return value for the set of parsed nodes
return nodes even if the data is broken (use 0)

simplesect.para: 0 if the chunk is well balanced, or thehe parser error code otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, int depth, const xmlChar *URL, const xmlChar *ID, xmlNode **lst)
memberdef.name: xmlParseExternalEntity

briefdescription.para: Parse an external general entity An external general parsed entity is well-formed if it matches the production labeled extParsedEnt.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses deprecated global variables to set parser options which default to XML_PARSE_NODICT.

parameterdescription.para: the document the chunk pertains to
The user data returned on SAX callbacks (possibly NULL)
the URL for the entity to load
the System ID for the entity to load
the return value for the set of parsed nodes

simplesect.para: 0 if the entity is well formed, -1 in case of args problem and the parser error code otherwise

briefdescription.para: Parse an external general entity within an existing parsing context An external general parsed entity is well-formed if it matches the production labeled extParsedEnt.

parameterdescription.para: the URL for the entity to load
the System ID for the entity to load
the return value for the set of parsed nodes

simplesect.para: 0 if the entity is well formed, -1 in case of args problem and the parser error code otherwise

briefdescription.para: Allocate and initialize a new parser context.

simplesect.para: the xmlParserCtxt or NULL

briefdescription.para: Allocate and initialize a new SAX parser context.

detaileddescription.para: If userData is NULL, the parser context will be passed as user data.

2.11.0
If you want support older versions, it's best to invoke xmlNewParserCtxt and set ctxt->sax with struct assignment.

simplesect.para: the xmlParserCtxt or NULL if memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a parser context.

xrefdescription.para: Internal function which will be made private in a future version.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Same as xmlCtxtReset.
Free all the memory used by a parser context.

detaileddescription.para: However the parsed document in ctxt->myDoc is not freed.

briefdescription.para: Setup the parser context to parse a new buffer; Clears any prior contents from the parser context.

detaileddescription.para: The buffer parameter must not be NULL, but the filename parameter can be

briefdescription.para: Creates a parser context for an XML in-memory document.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of xmlChar

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, const char *chunk, int size, const char *filename)
memberdef.name: xmlCreatePushParserCtxt

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for using the XML parser in push mode.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlParseChunk.
Passing an initial chunk is useless and deprecated.
The push parser doesn't support recovery mode or the XML_PARSE_NOBLANKS option.
filename is used as base URI to fetch external entities and for error reports.

parameterdescription.para: size of initial chunk in bytes

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *chunk, int size, int terminate)
memberdef.name: xmlParseChunk

briefdescription.para: Parse a chunk of memory in push parser mode.

detaileddescription.para: Assumes that the parser context was initialized with xmlCreatePushParserCtxt.

The last chunk, which will often be empty, must be marked with the terminate flag. With the default SAX callbacks, the resulting document will be available in ctxt->myDoc. This pointer will not be freed when calling xmlFreeParserCtxt and must be freed by the caller. If the document isn't well-formed, it will still be returned in ctxt->myDoc.
As an exception, xmlCtxtResetPush will free the document in ctxt->myDoc. So ctxt->myDoc should be set to NULL after extracting the document.
Since 2.14.0, xmlCtxtGetDocument can be used to retrieve the result document.

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code (0 on success).

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlSAXHandler *sax, void *user_data, xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, xmlCharEncoding enc)
memberdef.name: xmlCreateIOParserCtxt

briefdescription.para: Create a parser context for using the XML parser with an existing I/O stream.

simplesect.para: the new parser context or NULL

briefdescription.para: Create a new input stream structure encapsulating the input into a stream suitable for the parser.

simplesect.para: the new input stream or NULL

briefdescription.para: Find the parser node info struct for a given node.

parameterdescription.para: an XML node within the tree

simplesect.para: an xmlParserNodeInfo block pointer or NULL

briefdescription.para: Initialize (set to initial state) node info sequence.
Clear (release memory and reinitialize) node info sequence.
Find the index that the info record for the given node is or should be at in a sorted sequence.

simplesect.para: a long indicating the position of the record

briefdescription.para: Insert node info record into the sorted sequence.
Changes the default external entity resolver function for the application.

xrefdescription.para: This is a global setting and not thread-safe. Use xmlCtxtSetResourceLoader or similar functions.

briefdescription.para: Get the default external entity resolver function for the application.

simplesect.para: the xmlExternalEntityLoader function pointer

briefdescription.para: URL is a filename or URL.

detaileddescription.para: If if contains the substring "://", it is assumed to be a Legacy Extended IRI. Otherwise, it is treated as a filesystem path.
publicId is an optional XML public ID, typically from a doctype declaration. It is used for catalog lookups.

If catalog lookup is enabled (default is yes) and URL or ID are found in system or local XML catalogs, URL is replaced with the result. Then the following resource loaders will be called if they were registered (in order of precedence):

listitem.para: the resource loader set with xmlCtxtSetResourceLoader
the global external entity loader set with xmlSetExternalEntityLoader (without catalog resolution, deprecated)
the per-thread xmlParserInputBufferCreateFilenameFunc set with xmlParserInputBufferCreateFilenameDefault (deprecated)

the default loader which will return
the result from a matching global input callback set with xmlRegisterInputCallbacks (deprecated)
a file opened from the filesystem, with automatic detection of compressed files if support is compiled in.

parameterdescription.para: the URL or system ID for the entity to load
the public ID for the entity to load (optional)
the context in which the entity is called or NULL

simplesect.para: the xmlParserInput or NULL

briefdescription.para: This function provides the current index of the parser relative to the start of the current entity.

detaileddescription.para: This function is computed in bytes from the beginning starting at zero and finishing at the size in byte of the file if parsing a file. The function is of constant cost if the input is UTF-8 but can be costly if run on non-UTF-8 input.

simplesect.para: the index in bytes from the beginning of the entity or -1 in case the index could not be computed.

briefdescription.para: Reset a parser context.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *chunk, int size, const char *filename, const char *encoding)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtResetPush

briefdescription.para: Reset a push parser context.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to an array of chars
number of chars in the array
an optional file name or URI

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and 1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Get the current options of the parser context.

simplesect.para: the current options set in the parser context, or -1 if ctxt is NULL.

briefdescription.para: Applies the options to the parser context.

detaileddescription.para: Unset options are cleared.

2.13.0
With older versions, you can use xmlCtxtUseOptions.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, the set of unknown or unimplemented options in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Applies the options to the parser context.

detaileddescription.para: The following options are never cleared and can only be enabled:

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, the set of unknown or unimplemented options in case of error.
the private application data.

briefdescription.para: Set the private application data.

simplesect.para: the local catalogs.

briefdescription.para: Set the local catalogs.

simplesect.para: the dictionary.

briefdescription.para: Set the dictionary.

detaileddescription.para: This should only be done immediately after creating a parser context.

simplesect.para: the SAX handler struct. This is not a copy and must not be freed. Handlers can be updated.

briefdescription.para: Set the SAX handler struct to a copy of sax.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.
the parsed document or NULL if a fatal error occurred when parsing. The document must be freed by the caller. Resets the context's document to NULL.
1 if this is a HTML parser context, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the parser is stopped.

detaileddescription.para: The parser is stopped on fatal (non-wellformedness) errors or on user request with xmlStopParser.

simplesect.para: 1 if the parser is stopped, 0 otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Check whether a DTD subset is being parsed.

detaileddescription.para: Should only be used by SAX callbacks.
Return values are

simplesect.para: the subset status
the validation context.
the version from the XML declaration.
the encoding from the encoding declaration. This can differ from the actual encoding.
the value from the standalone document declaration.

briefdescription.para: Get well-formedness and validation status after parsing.

detaileddescription.para: Also reports catastrophic errors which are not related to parsing like out-of-memory, I/O or other errors.

simplesect.para: a bitmask of XML_STATUS_* flags ORed together.

briefdescription.para: Return user data.

detaileddescription.para: Return user data of a custom SAX parser or the parser context itself if unset.

simplesect.para: the user data.

briefdescription.para: Return the current node being parsed.

detaileddescription.para: This is only useful if the default SAX callbacks which build a document tree are intercepted. This mode of operation is fragile and discouraged.
Returns the current element node, or the document node if no element was parsed yet.

simplesect.para: the current node.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const xmlChar **name, const xmlChar **systemId, const xmlChar **publicId)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtGetDocTypeDecl

briefdescription.para: Return data from the doctype declaration.

detaileddescription.para: Should only be used by SAX callbacks.

parameterdescription.para: system ID (URI) of the external subset (output)
public ID of the external subset (output)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if argument is invalid

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, int inputIndex, const char **filname, int *line, int *col, unsigned long *bytePos)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtGetInputPosition

briefdescription.para: Return input position.

detaileddescription.para: Should only be used by error handlers or SAX callbacks.

Because of entities, there can be multiple inputs. Non-negative values of inputIndex (0, 1, 2, ...) select inputs starting from the outermost input. Negative values (-1, -2, ...) select inputs starting from the innermost input.
The byte position is counted in possibly decoded UTF-8 bytes, so it won't match the position in the raw input data.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if arguments are invalid

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, int inputIndex, const xmlChar **startOut, int *sizeInOut, int *offsetOut)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtGetInputWindow

briefdescription.para: Return window into input data.

detaileddescription.para: Should only be used by error handlers or SAX callbacks. The returned pointer is only valid until the callback returns.

Because of entities, there can be multiple inputs. Non-negative values of inputIndex (0, 1, 2, ...) select inputs starting from the outermost input. Negative values (-1, -2, ...) select inputs starting from the innermost input.

parameterdescription.para: maximum size of window (in) actual size of window (out)
offset of current position inside window (out)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if arguments are invalid

briefdescription.para: Register a callback function that will be called on errors and warnings.

detaileddescription.para: If handler is NULL, the error handler will be deactivated.
If you only want to disable parser errors being printed to stderr, use xmlParserOption XML_PARSE_NOERROR.
This is the recommended way to collect errors from the parser and takes precedence over all other error reporting mechanisms. These are (in order of precedence):

briefdescription.para: Installs a custom callback to load documents, DTDs or external entities.

detaileddescription.para: If vctxt is NULL, the parser context will be passed.

briefdescription.para: Installs a custom implementation to convert between character encodings.

detaileddescription.para: This bypasses legacy feature like global encoding handlers or encoding aliases.

briefdescription.para: To protect against exponential entity expansion ("billion laughs"), the size of serialized output is (roughly) limited to the input size multiplied by this factor.

detaileddescription.para: The default value is 5.
When working with documents making heavy use of entity expansion, it can be necessary to increase the value. For security reasons, this should only be considered when processing trusted input.

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlChar *cur, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReadDoc

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an XML document from a zero-terminated string.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlCtxtReadDoc for details.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a zero terminated string

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Convenience function to parse an XML file from the filesystem or a global, user-defined resource loader.

detaileddescription.para: If a "-" filename is passed, the function will read from stdin. This feature is potentially insecure and might be removed from later versions.
See xmlCtxtReadFile for details.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *buffer, int size, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReadMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: The input buffer must not contain a terminating null byte.
See xmlCtxtReadMemory for details.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (int fd, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReadFd

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML from a file descriptor and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlCtxtReadFd for details.
NOTE that the file descriptor will not be closed when the context is freed or reset.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlReadIO

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document from I/O functions and context and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlCtxtReadIO for details.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document and return the resulting document tree.

detaileddescription.para: Takes ownership of the input object.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse a well-balanced chunk of XML matching the 'content' production.

detaileddescription.para: Namespaces in scope of node and entities of node's document are recognized. When validating, the DTD of node's document is used.
Always consumes input even in error case.

simplesect.para: a node list or NULL in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const xmlChar *cur, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadDoc

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: URL is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a zero terminated string

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *filename, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadFile

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML file from the filesystem or a global, user-defined resource loader.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, const char *buffer, int size, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadMemory

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML in-memory document and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: The input buffer must not contain a terminating null byte.
URL is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a char array

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, int fd, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadFd

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document from a file descriptor and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE that the file descriptor will not be closed when the context is freed or reset.
URL is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlParserCtxt *ctxt, xmlInputReadCallback ioread, xmlInputCloseCallback ioclose, void *ioctx, const char *URL, const char *encoding, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlCtxtReadIO

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML document from I/O functions and source and build a tree.

detaileddescription.para: This reuses the existing ctxt parser context
URL is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.

parameterdescription.para: the base URL to use for the document

simplesect.para: the resulting document tree

briefdescription.para: Create a new input stream based on a file or a URL.

detaileddescription.para: The flag XML_INPUT_UNZIP allows decompression.
The flag XML_INPUT_NETWORK allows network access.
The following resource loaders will be called if they were registered (in order of precedence):

listitem.para: the per-thread xmlParserInputBufferCreateFilenameFunc set with xmlParserInputBufferCreateFilenameDefault (deprecated)

the default loader which will return
the result from a matching global input callback set with xmlRegisterInputCallbacks (deprecated)
a file opened from the filesystem, with automatic detection of compressed files if support is compiled in.

parameterdescription.para: the filename to use as entity

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *url, const void *mem, size_t size, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlNewInputFromMemory

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input to read from a memory area.

detaileddescription.para: url is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.
If the XML_INPUT_BUF_STATIC flag is set, the memory area must stay unchanged until parsing has finished. This can avoid temporary copies.
If the XML_INPUT_BUF_ZERO_TERMINATED flag is set, the memory area must contain a zero byte after the buffer at position size. This can avoid temporary copies.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input to read from a zero-terminated string.

detaileddescription.para: url is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.
If the XML_INPUT_BUF_STATIC flag is set, the string must stay unchanged until parsing has finished. This can avoid temporary copies.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input to read from a file descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: url is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.
fd is closed after parsing has finished.
Supported flags are XML_INPUT_UNZIP to decompress data automatically. This feature is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (const char *url, xmlInputReadCallback ioRead, xmlInputCloseCallback ioClose, void *ioCtxt, xmlParserInputFlags flags)
memberdef.name: xmlNewInputFromIO

briefdescription.para: Creates a new parser input to read from input callbacks and context.

detaileddescription.para: url is used as base to resolve external entities and for error reporting.
ioRead is called to read new data into a provided buffer. It must return the number of bytes written into the buffer ot a negative xmlParserErrors code on failure.
ioClose is called after parsing has finished.
ioCtxt is an opaque pointer passed to the callbacks.

simplesect.para: a new parser input or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Use encoding handler to decode input data.

detaileddescription.para: Closes the handler on error.

simplesect.para: an xmlParserErrors code.

briefdescription.para: Examines if the library has been compiled with a given feature.

simplesect.para: zero (0) if the feature does not exist or an unknown feature is requested, non-zero otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Validating XML 1.0 parser.

detaileddescription.para: Interfaces, constants and types related to the XML parser.

simplesect.para: See Copyright for the status of this software.
Daniel Veillard
